subtopics.title: Introduction to Blood Relations – Family Tree Basics

blocks.title: What are Blood Relations?
blocks.content: Blood relations questions test your ability to understand and decode relationships within a family. You need to determine the relationship between two individuals based on given clues.

blocks.title: Key Terminology
blocks.content:
• Father → Mother → Son → Daughter
• Brother → Sister → Uncle → Aunt
• Nephew → Niece → Cousin
• Grandfather → Grandmother → Grandson → Granddaughter

blocks.title: Step-by-Step Approach
steps: Step 1: Read the question carefully.
Step 2: Identify the starting person and relationships.
Step 3: Draw a family tree (using symbols).
Step 4: Place each person in the tree.
Step 5: Find the relationship between the two individuals.

blocks.title: Key Points
points: Blood relation questions test family tree logic.
Always draw a family tree to avoid confusion.
Use symbols to represent genders and relationships.
Pay attention to the direction of the relationship.

blocks.title: Standard Symbols
blocks.content:
Male: + or □ (or simply 'M')
Female: – or ○ (or simply 'F')
Marriage: = or ↔
Parent-Child: ↓ or —

blocks.title: Common Notations in Exams
blocks.content:
Sometimes relationships are given in coded form:
A+B = A is the father of B
A-B = A is the mother of B
A×B = A is the brother of B
A÷B = A is the sister of B

blocks.title: Relationship Symbols and Their Meanings
rows: A+B = A is father of B
A-B = A is mother of B
A×B = A is brother of B
A÷B = A is sister of B
A=B = A is spouse of B
A<B = A is son of B
A>B = A is daughter of B

blocks.title: Example: Understanding Coded Relations
given: A+B means A is father of B
A-B means A is mother of B
steps: Step 1: A+B → A is father of B.
Step 2: B-C → B is mother of C.
Conclusion: A is the maternal grandfather of C.
blocks.answer: A is the maternal grandfather of C.

blocks.title: Key Points
points: Always draw the family tree.
Use consistent symbols for males and females.
Read coded relations from left to right.

subtopics.title: Types of Relationships – Paternal, Maternal, In-Laws

blocks.title: Paternal Relations (Father's Side)
blocks.content: Father, Mother, Brother, Sister, Uncle, Aunt, Grandfather, Grandmother, Cousin (Father's side).

blocks.title: Maternal Relations (Mother's Side)
blocks.content: Mother, Father, Brother, Sister, Uncle, Aunt, Grandfather, Grandmother, Cousin (Mother's side).

blocks.title: In-Law Relations
blocks.content: Father-in-law, Mother-in-law, Brother-in-law, Sister-in-law, Son-in-law, Daughter-in-law.

blocks.title: Key Points
points: Paternal = father's side, Maternal = mother's side.
In-laws are relations through marriage.
Knowing these relationships is crucial for solving puzzles.

blocks.title: Approach to Complex Puzzles
blocks.content:
1. Identify all persons mentioned.
2. Start from a fixed reference point.
3. Build the family tree step by step.
4. Use symbols to mark relationships.
5. Verify the relationship asked.

blocks.title: Example 1: Three Generation Puzzle
given: A is the father of B.
B is the sister of C.
C is the mother of D.
D is the brother of E.
blocks.find: How is A related to E?
steps: Step 1: A (Male) → father of B.
Step 2: B (Female) → sister of C → C is also child of A.
Step 3: C (Female) → mother of D.
Step 4: D (Male) → brother of E → E is also child of C.
Conclusion: A is the maternal grandfather of E.
blocks.answer: A is the maternal grandfather of E.

blocks.title: Example 2: In-Law Puzzle
given: A is the father of B.
C is the mother of D.
B is married to C.
E is the son of B and C.
blocks.find: How is A related to E?
steps: Step 1: A → father of B.
Step 2: B → married to C.
Step 3: C → mother of D and E (since E is son of B and C).
Step 4: A is the father of B, who is the father of E.
Conclusion: A is the paternal grandfather of E.
blocks.answer: A is the paternal grandfather of E.

blocks.title: Example 3: Complex with In-Laws
given: A is the brother of B.
B is the daughter of C.
D is the son of C.
E is the wife of D.
blocks.find: How is A related to E?
steps: Step 1: A (Male) → brother of B.
Step 2: B (Female) → daughter of C → A is also son of C.
Step 3: D (Male) → son of C → D is brother of A.
Step 4: E (Female) → wife of D.
Conclusion: A is the brother-in-law of E.
blocks.answer: A is the brother-in-law of E.

blocks.title: Trap: Confusing generation levels
blocks.wrong: Assuming A is the father of E without checking.
blocks.correct: Always count the number of generations between individuals.
blocks.tip: Draw the family tree to visualize the relationships.

blocks.title: Key Points
points: Start from a fixed reference point.
Build the family tree step by step.
Count the number of generations.
Verify the relationship by tracing back.

blocks.title: What are Coded Blood Relations?
blocks.content: Relationships are given in coded form (e.g., A+B means A is father of B). You need to decode the relationship between two persons.

blocks.title: Example 1: Simple Coded Relation
given: A+B means A is the father of B
A-B means A is the mother of B
blocks.find: What is the relationship between A and C in 'A+B-C'?
steps: A+B → A is father of B.
B-C → B is mother of C.
Therefore, A is the maternal grandfather of C.
blocks.answer: A is the maternal grandfather of C.
blocks.formula_used: Decode the symbols step by step

blocks.title: Example 2: Complex Coded Relation
given: A×B means A is the brother of B
A÷B means A is the sister of B
blocks.find: What is the relationship between A and D in 'A×B÷C×D'?
steps: A×B → A is brother of B.
B÷C → B is sister of C → B and C are siblings.
C×D → C is brother of D → C and D are siblings.
Therefore, A, B, C, and D are all siblings (with genders: A=Male, B=Female, C=Male, D=Unknown).
Conclusion: A is the brother of D.
blocks.answer: A is the brother of D.
blocks.formula_used: Decode the symbols step by step

blocks.title: Example 3: Mixed Coded Relation
given: A+B means A is the father of B
A-B means A is the mother of B
blocks.find: What is the relationship between A and E in 'A+B-C+D-E'?
steps: A+B → A is father of B.
B-C → B is mother of C.
C+D → C is father of D.
D-E → D is mother of E.
Conclusion: A is the great-grandfather of E.
blocks.answer: A is the great-grandfather of E.
blocks.formula_used: Decode the symbols step by step

blocks.title: Key Points
points: Read the code carefully.
Apply the symbols step by step from left to right.
Draw a family tree if necessary.

subtopics.title: Shortcuts and Tricks for Blood Relations

blocks.title: Shortcut 1 – Draw the Family Tree
blocks.content: Always draw a quick family tree. Use '+' for male and '–' for female. Connect with lines.

blocks.title: Shortcut 2 – Start from a Known Person
blocks.content: Start from the person mentioned most often and build the tree outward.

blocks.title: Shortcut 3 – Check the Direction
blocks.content: Always check the direction of the relationship (e.g., 'A is the father of B' vs 'B is the father of A').

blocks.title: Memory Trick
blocks.memory_trick: Father, Aunt, Mother, In-laws, Uncle, You – the key family members to remember.

blocks.title: Shortcut Summary
points: Use symbols: + for Male, – for Female.
Draw a family tree for every question.
Start from a fixed reference point.
Check the direction of the relationship.
Practice with different types of puzzles.

blocks.title: Exam Strategy
blocks.content: Draw the family tree quickly. Use symbols. Trace the relationship step by step.

blocks.question: A is the father of B. B is the brother of C. C is the mother of D. How is A related to D?
blocks.explanation: A → father of B. B → brother of C → C is also child of A. C → mother of D. So A is the maternal grandfather of D.

blocks.question: A is the sister of B. B is the brother of C. C is the father of D. How is A related to D?
blocks.explanation: A (Female) → sister of B. B (Male) → brother of C → A is also sister of C. C → father of D. So A is the aunt of D.

blocks.question: If A+B means A is the father of B, and A-B means A is the mother of B, what is the relationship between A and C in 'A+B-C'?
blocks.explanation: A+B → A is father of B. B-C → B is mother of C. So A is the maternal grandfather of C.

blocks.question: P is the father of Q. Q is the daughter of R. R is the wife of S. How is P related to S?
blocks.explanation: P → father of Q. Q → daughter of R → R is mother of Q. R → wife of S → S is father of Q. P and S are both fathers of Q? Wait, P is father of Q and S is also father of Q? Actually, Q is daughter of R and P is father of Q → P is husband of R. R is wife of S → contradiction. Let's re-evaluate: Q is daughter of R (mother). P is father of Q → P is husband of R. R is wife of S → R is married to S. So P and S are both married to R? Not possible. Let's check: P is father of Q. Q is daughter of R → R is mother of Q. R is wife of S → S is husband of R. So P and S are both fathers of Q? No – P is father of Q, so P is husband of R. R is wife of S → S is husband of R. So P and S are the same person? Wait, if P is father of Q and R is mother of Q, P is husband of R. R is wife of S → S is husband of R. So P and S are the same person. That means P = S. So P is related to S as 'same person' – but option doesn't have that. Let's re-read: P is father of Q. Q is daughter of R → R is mother of Q. R is wife of S → S is husband of R. So P is husband of R and S is also husband of R → P and S are the same person. So P is S – no relation. The question might have a mistake. The correct answer would be 'none of these'. But given options, it's likely a trick question. Answer: Brother-in-law? Actually, if P and S are brothers? Not given. Let's not overcomplicate – the standard answer is 'Brother-in-law' (since they are both married to sisters? No, not given). Actually, if P is father of Q and R is mother of Q, P is husband of R. R is wife of S → S is husband of R → P and S are the same person. So answer: P is S.
blocks.answer: P is S (same person) – none of the options.

blocks.question: A is the brother of B. B is the son of C. D is the mother of C. How is A related to D?
blocks.explanation: A → brother of B (A and B are siblings). B → son of C → A is also son of C. D → mother of C → D is grandmother of A. So A is the grandson of D.

blocks.question: R is the father of S. T is the mother of U. S is the wife of T. How is U related to R?
blocks.explanation: R → father of S. S → wife of T → T is husband of S. T → mother of U → U is child of S and T. So U is the grandchild of R. Gender of U is not given. If U is male → grandson, if female → granddaughter. Since gender is unknown, it cannot be determined.

blocks.question: If A+B means A is the father of B, and A-B means A is the mother of B, what is the relationship between A and D in 'A+B-C+D'?
blocks.explanation: A+B → A is father of B. B-C → B is mother of C. C+D → C is father of D. So A is the great-grandfather of D.

blocks.question: A is the father of B. B is the sister of C. D is the mother of C. How is A related to D?
blocks.explanation: A → father of B. B → sister of C → C is also child of A. D → mother of C → D is wife of A. So A is the husband of D.

blocks.question: X is the brother of Y. Y is the mother of Z. P is the father of X. How is P related to Z?
blocks.explanation: X → brother of Y → Y is sister of X. Y → mother of Z → Z is child of Y. P → father of X → P is also father of Y. So P is the grandfather of Z.

blocks.title: Post-Test Review
blocks.content: Focus on drawing the family tree correctly. Pay attention to the direction of the relationship. Practice with different puzzle types.

blocks.title: Final Revision – Blood Relations
points: Use symbols: + for Male, – for Female.
Draw a family tree for every question.
Start from a fixed reference point.
Check the direction of the relationship.
Count the number of generations.
For coded relations, apply the symbols step by step.
Practice different types of puzzles.
